meta.title: Who's to Say Sports Media | Who's to Say ? Foundation
meta.description: Sports storytelling, fan culture, media projects, and community connection with clear boundaries around event costs and support services.

hero.title: Who's to Say Sports Media
hero.subtitle: Sports storytelling, fan culture, interviews, recaps, and media projects — with event costs, transportation, and support handled separately when needed.
hero.bestFor: People who love sports, teams, fan culture, interviews, photography, recaps, or sports storytelling.

blocks.html: Who's to Say Sports Media uses sports as a setting for storytelling, communication, creative media, and community connection.
Participants may create game recaps, fan interviews, trading-card concepts, commentary clips, photo stories, or short videos around teams and sports experiences they care about.
This creative-media lane is separate from athletic instruction, event admission, transportation, supervision, or direct support.

items: Recording short game recaps or fan takes
Interviewing teammates, coaches, family members, volunteers, or friends with consent
Filming highlights or behind-the-scenes moments on a phone
Creating simple sports commentary, podcast, or social media-style segments
Reviewing clips and practicing clear communication
Building a personal sports voice, brand, or storytelling style
Taking team or event photos when permission is clear
Creating trading-card concepts, player spotlights, or recap graphics
Preparing a small portfolio or story package
Preparing questions for interviews
Practicing introductions and appreciation for coaches or volunteers
Reflecting afterward on what went well and what story should be told next

sections.title: What This Lane Does Not Include

items: Athletic instruction, conditioning plans, workout coaching, or nutrition coaching
Admission tickets, entertainment costs, or unrelated purchases
Transportation, supervision, or community accompaniment unless separately arranged through the proper support lane
Using participant work mainly as Foundation marketing or fundraising without clear consent and ownership terms
Communication and interview skills
Planning, sequencing, and follow-through
Creative media and storytelling skills
Confidence participating in sports culture as a fan, storyteller, photographer, or media helper
Participant-owned photos, clips, recaps, or story assets

blocks.html: Who's to Say Sports Media is open to people who are interested in sports, fan culture, community storytelling, or sports media. Participants do not need to be competitive athletes.

sessionStyle.title: Session Style
items: Sports media & storytelling projects
Individual creator projects or small groups
Direct support and event costs handled separately

getStarted.title: Get Started
getStarted.prompt: Interested in a sports story, recap, interview, or fan-media project?

blocks.html: A sports-media project starts with the participant's interest: a team, player, memory, event, or role. We then define the creative output, consent needs, session plan, and any separate support arrangements needed for community settings.
